name: Deploy New Model to Inference
description: An MLOps engineer is tasked with deploying a newly trained machine learning model to the production inference environment. The goal is to ensure the new model version is safely released, with monitoring and validation steps to confirm correct operation. This process must follow safe deployment practices and maintain the integrity of confidential production data.

steps.action: Review deployment requirements and confirm model artifact integrity
steps.verify: Artifact checksum matches and deployment checklist is signed off

steps.action: Validate new model against a holdout test dataset in staging
steps.verify: Test results align with acceptance criteria documented in the ticket

steps.action: Update deployment manifests to reference the new model version
steps.verify: Manifest files are updated and committed to the version control system

steps.action: Obtain manager approval for production deployment
steps.verify: Approval is recorded in the change management system

steps.action: Schedule a maintenance window for deployment
steps.verify: Deployment window is visible on the team calendar

steps.action: Deploy the new model to a canary subset of production traffic
steps.verify: Canary deployment status is reported as healthy in the monitoring dashboard

steps.action: Monitor canary metrics for performance and error rates
steps.verify: No anomalies or regression alerts are observed in metrics

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of canary results and intent to proceed
steps.verify: Notification is acknowledged in the team communication channel

steps.action: Roll out the new model to full production traffic
steps.verify: Production deployment status is healthy and serving live requests

steps.action: Monitor post-deployment metrics and logs for issues
steps.verify: No critical errors or performance regressions are detected

steps.action: Update model registry with new version and deployment metadata
steps.verify: Model registry entry reflects current version and deployment timestamp

steps.action: Document deployment steps and validation results in the operations log
steps.verify: Operations log entry is complete and accessible to the team

abort_conditions: Artifact validation fails or integrity cannot be confirmed
Manager approval is denied or not received
Canary deployment shows regression or critical errors

name: Production IAM Security Audit
description: A security engineer performs a comprehensive audit of Identity and Access Management (IAM) configurations in the production environment. The goal is to identify misconfigurations, excessive permissions, and potential security risks, ensuring compliance with internal and regulatory security standards. The process includes review, documentation, and reporting for follow-up actions.

steps.action: Review current user and service account inventory in production IAM.
steps.verify: Inventory list is complete and up to date.

steps.action: Document all privileged roles and their assignments.
steps.verify: Privileged role mapping is documented and reviewed.

steps.action: Analyze group memberships for excessive privilege escalation paths.
steps.verify: Potential privilege escalation paths are flagged in the audit report.

steps.action: Check for orphaned accounts or unused credentials.
steps.verify: List of orphaned or unused accounts is created.

steps.action: Review recent IAM configuration changes in production.
steps.verify: Change log is reviewed and anomalies are noted.

steps.action: Assess adherence to least privilege principles for all roles.
steps.verify: Roles with excessive permissions are identified.

steps.action: Cross-reference access logs for unusual activity related to IAM.
steps.verify: Unusual activity is noted in the audit findings.

steps.action: Check compliance of IAM settings with internal and regulatory policies.
steps.verify: Compliance checklist is completed.

steps.action: Identify accounts lacking multi-factor authentication.
steps.verify: List of accounts without MFA is generated.

steps.action: Review third-party integrations with production IAM.
steps.verify: Documentation of integrations is updated and reviewed.

steps.action: Validate IAM audit logging is enabled and functioning.
steps.verify: Audit log status is confirmed.

steps.action: Summarize all findings and risk areas in an audit report.
steps.verify: Audit report draft is completed.

steps.action: Share the draft report with relevant security and compliance stakeholders.
steps.verify: Stakeholders acknowledge report receipt.

steps.action: Document recommended remediation actions for identified risks.
steps.verify: Remediation plan is attached to the audit report.

steps.action: Archive audit artifacts in a secure documentation repository.
steps.verify: Artifacts are stored and access is restricted.

steps.action: Schedule follow-up review meeting with security and operations teams.
steps.verify: Meeting is scheduled and calendar invite is sent.

abort_conditions: IAM inventory is incomplete or inaccessible
Access to audit logs is denied
Verification identifies unsafe or non-compliant environment

name: Routine Database Maintenance
description: A site reliability engineer performs scheduled maintenance on the production database to ensure optimal performance and reliability. The process is conducted with production-admin access and must not disrupt live services. All actions are logged and verified to comply with internal controls.

steps.action: Review the maintenance schedule and confirm the maintenance window with stakeholders
steps.verify: Maintenance window is acknowledged in the change calendar

steps.action: Notify affected teams about the upcoming database maintenance
steps.verify: Notification is visible in team communication channels

steps.action: Check current database health metrics and active connections
steps.verify: Health metrics are recorded and show normal operation

steps.action: Ensure recent backups are available and validated
steps.verify: Backup status report confirms successful backup and integrity check

steps.action: Place the database in maintenance mode if supported
steps.verify: Maintenance mode status is reflected in the database dashboard

steps.action: Apply scheduled maintenance tasks such as index optimization and log rotation
steps.verify: Maintenance log reflects successful completion of tasks

steps.action: Monitor database performance metrics during maintenance activities
steps.verify: Performance metrics remain within expected thresholds

steps.action: Remove the database from maintenance mode
steps.verify: Database is confirmed to be back in normal operation mode

steps.action: Notify stakeholders that maintenance is complete
steps.verify: Post-maintenance notification is visible to all relevant teams

steps.action: Document the maintenance activities and any findings in the operational log
steps.verify: Maintenance record is complete and accessible in documentation system

abort_conditions: Backup validation fails
Database health check indicates critical issues
Unexpected errors occur during maintenance steps

name: Rollback Production Deployment
description: Initiate and execute a rollback of a production deployment in Kubernetes following a failed release. The goal is to restore application stability while minimizing downtime and data loss. This process follows established safety and approval protocols to ensure production integrity.

steps.action: Review the release notes and incident reports to confirm the need for rollback
steps.verify: Incident documentation and release logs confirm rollback requirement

steps.action: Notify the oncall manager and request explicit approval to proceed with rollback
steps.verify: Manager approval is received and documented in the incident ticket

steps.action: Communicate planned rollback to impacted stakeholders and affected teams
steps.verify: Stakeholders acknowledge receipt of rollback notification

steps.action: Identify the last stable deployment version to target for rollback
steps.verify: Stable deployment version is documented in the change management system

steps.action: Validate current cluster health and resource availability before initiating rollback
steps.verify: Cluster health metrics and monitoring dashboards show no critical issues

steps.action: Update deployment configuration to reference the last stable version
steps.verify: Configuration management system reflects the correct deployment version

steps.action: Initiate the rollback process in the production Kubernetes cluster
steps.verify: Deployment status in the cluster dashboard shows rollback in progress

steps.action: Monitor pod restarts, scaling events, and service endpoints during rollback
steps.verify: Pod status and service endpoints stabilize at expected configuration

steps.action: Check application logs and error rates for signs of recovery or continuing issues
steps.verify: Application logs and metrics indicate error rates are returning to normal

steps.action: Validate that all user-facing services are functional and meet baseline SLAs
steps.verify: Synthetic monitoring and end-user tests confirm service availability

steps.action: Communicate rollback completion and new deployment status to stakeholders
steps.verify: Stakeholders confirm receipt of rollback completion update

steps.action: Document the rollback process, outcomes, and lessons learned in the incident report
steps.verify: Incident report is updated and approved in the incident management system

abort_conditions: Manager approval for rollback is denied
Cluster health checks fail prior to rollback
Critical verification step fails or new incidents are detected during rollback

The process ensures all marketing activities adhere to company and legal standards.

steps.verify: Campaign list is current and complete in the documentation repository

steps.verify: All required data fields are populated and match source systems

steps.action: Review campaign content for adherence to company and regulatory guidelines
steps.verify: Compliance checklist is fully completed for each campaign

steps.action: Document any compliance incidents or exceptions found during the review
steps.verify: Incident log is updated and referenced in the draft report

steps.action: Summarize compliance status and findings for all campaigns
steps.verify: Summary section in the report accurately reflects findings

steps.action: Draft the quarterly compliance report in the documentation system
steps.verify: Draft report is saved and accessible in the docs_wiki

steps.action: Share the draft report with the marketing manager for review
steps.verify: Notification sent and report access granted to the manager

steps.action: Incorporate manager feedback and finalize the compliance report
steps.verify: Final report reflects all required changes and is marked as complete

steps.action: Archive the finalized compliance report in the designated docs_wiki folder
steps.verify: Report is stored in the correct location with proper access controls

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of report completion and location
steps.verify: Stakeholders confirm receipt of notification and access to the report

abort_conditions: Required campaign data is incomplete or unavailable
Compliance incident cannot be resolved or documented
Manager approval for report submission is denied

name: Handle Customer Escalation
description: A customer success manager addresses a high-priority customer escalation. This includes reviewing the ticket details, coordinating with support, and ensuring timely resolution with proper documentation. Data involved is confidential and actions must be traceable in the CRM.

steps.action: Acknowledge receipt of the escalation ticket in the CRM system
steps.verify: Ticket status updated to acknowledged and customer notified

steps.action: Review all available ticket details, customer notes, and previous communications

steps.action: Prioritize the escalation according to urgency and business impact
steps.verify: Ticket priority field set to high and visible in the CRM

steps.action: Assign dedicated support resources to the escalation

steps.action: Contact the customer to communicate next steps and expected timelines

steps.action: Coordinate with technical or product teams for required investigation or resolution steps

steps.verify: Resolution summary reviewed and documented in the CRM

steps.action: Communicate the resolution to the customer and confirm satisfaction
steps.verify: Customer confirmation and feedback logged in the CRM

steps.action: Close the escalation ticket and update resolution notes
steps.verify: Ticket status set to closed and resolution details saved

steps.action: Document key learnings and update internal knowledge base if applicable
steps.verify: Knowledge base article or internal note updated with escalation insights

abort_conditions: Verification of customer communication fails
Manager approval for escalation is denied

name: Project Status Update
description: The project manager prepares and submits a project status update for ongoing delivery work. This process includes gathering progress data, updating documentation, and notifying stakeholders through official channels.

steps.action: Collect the latest progress reports from team leads
steps.verify: All relevant reports are received and logged in the tracking document

steps.action: Review current project milestones and deadlines
steps.verify: Milestone and deadline statuses are confirmed in the project tracker

steps.action: Summarize key accomplishments since the last update

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of the updated status via official communication channel
steps.verify: Notification is sent and receipt is confirmed in the communication log

steps.action: Archive previous status update for recordkeeping
steps.verify: Previous status document is moved to the designated archive location

name: Triage Production Alert
description: A site reliability engineer on call receives a high-urgency alert from the monitoring system affecting production. The engineer must triage the alert, assess impact, escalate as needed, and ensure system health is restored or stabilized. This process involves systematic data gathering, communication, and validation steps to resolve or mitigate the incident.

steps.verify: Alert status updated to acknowledged in dashboard

steps.action: Review alert details and associated metadata
steps.verify: Alert summary and metadata displayed in monitoring tool

steps.action: Check related dashboards for system and application health indicators
steps.verify: Dashboards display current metrics and trends

steps.action: Identify affected services and components
steps.verify: List of impacted services/components documented in incident log

steps.action: Assess alert severity and potential customer impact
steps.verify: Severity and impact assessment recorded in incident ticket

steps.action: Communicate initial findings to incident response channel
steps.verify: Incident channel contains update with findings

steps.action: Correlate alert with recent changes or deployments
steps.verify: Change log or deployment history reviewed and noted

steps.action: Gather relevant logs and error traces for deeper analysis
steps.verify: Log and trace data attached to incident documentation

steps.action: Check for similar or related alerts in the recent past
steps.verify: History of related alerts reviewed and summarized

steps.action: Escalate to relevant engineering teams if root cause is outside SRE scope
steps.verify: Escalation documented and responsible parties notified

steps.action: Monitor for alert recurrence or new correlated alerts
steps.verify: Ongoing alert status monitored and tracked in dashboard

steps.action: Update incident ticket with ongoing status and actions taken
steps.verify: Incident ticket reflects real-time status and actions

steps.action: Communicate status updates to stakeholders on defined intervals
steps.verify: Stakeholder communication logs updated

steps.action: Confirm mitigation or resolution steps have restored system health
steps.verify: System and service metrics return to normal thresholds

steps.action: Document lessons learned and improvement actions
steps.verify: Post-incident documentation completed and shared

steps.action: Close the alert and incident ticket after verification
steps.verify: Incident ticket and alert status marked as resolved

abort_conditions: Verification of system health fails after mitigation
Incident ticket approval or required escalation is denied
Production environment is determined to be unsafe for further action

name: Restore Database from Backup After Incident
description: A site reliability engineer is tasked with restoring the production database from the most recent backup following a critical incident. This process requires careful validation of backup integrity and coordination with relevant teams to ensure a safe and compliant recovery. The operation is urgent and must comply with regulated data handling requirements.

steps.verify: Incident ticket status updated to show restoration in progress

steps.action: Notify relevant stakeholders about the planned database restoration
steps.verify: Notifications sent and receipt confirmed by stakeholders

steps.action: Obtain two-person approval for destructive recovery actions
steps.verify: Approval documented in change management system

steps.action: Identify and select the most recent valid backup suitable for restoration
steps.verify: Backup selection documented and validated in backup inventory

steps.action: Validate backup integrity and completeness
steps.verify: Backup validation report reviewed and confirmed

steps.action: Isolate the affected database environment to prevent further changes
steps.verify: Database access logs show environment is isolated

steps.action: Communicate downtime window and expected impact to all affected teams
steps.verify: Downtime notice acknowledged by all teams

steps.action: Initiate the database restoration process from the selected backup
steps.verify: Restoration progress tracked and status updated in ticket

steps.action: Monitor system logs and metrics for errors or anomalies during restoration
steps.verify: No critical errors appear in real-time monitoring dashboards

steps.action: Validate data integrity and consistency after restoration completes
steps.verify: Data integrity checks and consistency reports indicate no discrepancies

steps.action: Coordinate with application owners to perform functional tests
steps.verify: Functional test results documented and reviewed

steps.action: Re-enable database environment for regular operations
steps.verify: Database access restored and confirmed operational

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of restoration completion and system status
steps.verify: Stakeholders confirm receipt of restoration completion notice

steps.action: Document the entire restoration process and update incident records
steps.verify: Post-incident documentation reviewed and closed in ticketing system

name: Perform Security Audit
description: A security engineer initiates and conducts a comprehensive security audit on identity and access management systems. steps.verify: Approval is documented in the ticketing system

steps.action: Collect current access control policies from the IAM system
steps.verify: Access policy documents are retrieved and stored in the audit workspace

steps.action: Review user roles and permissions for excessive or outdated privileges
steps.verify: List of roles with excessive or outdated permissions is generated

steps.action: Analyze audit logs for suspicious access patterns over the past quarter
steps.verify: Summary report of anomalous access patterns is compiled

steps.action: Validate multi-factor authentication enforcement on privileged accounts
steps.verify: MFA status for all privileged accounts is included in the audit report

steps.action: Check for accounts that have not been used in over 90 days
steps.verify: Inactive account list is generated and reviewed

steps.action: Review service accounts for least privilege adherence
steps.verify: Service account privileges are mapped and deviations noted

steps.action: Verify that password policies meet compliance requirements
steps.verify: Password policy configuration is compared with compliance standards

steps.action: Inspect recent changes to IAM configuration for unauthorized modifications
steps.verify: Change log audit is completed and discrepancies highlighted

steps.action: Document findings and categorize them by risk level
steps.verify: Findings document includes risk categories for each issue

steps.action: Draft recommendations for remediation of identified issues
steps.verify: Recommendations section is added to the audit report

steps.action: Share preliminary audit report with security team for peer review
steps.verify: Peer review feedback is received and documented

steps.action: Incorporate feedback and finalize the security audit report

name: Kubernetes Release Rollback
description: A site reliability engineer initiates a rollback of a recent Kubernetes release in the production environment in response to a critical incident. The process involves validating the current state, coordinating with stakeholders, executing the rollback, and verifying system stability post-rollback. This ensures service restoration to a known good state while following safe operational practices.

steps.action: Review the incident report and confirm the affected services and release version.
steps.verify: Incident ticket contains clear description and affected components are identified.

steps.action: Notify relevant stakeholders about the planned rollback and expected impact.
steps.verify: Stakeholders acknowledge notification via incident communication channel.

steps.action: Obtain manager approval for the rollback as required by policy.
steps.verify: Approval is documented in the incident management system.

steps.action: Assess the current state of the Kubernetes cluster and health of critical workloads.
steps.verify: Cluster and workloads status is captured in monitoring dashboard.

steps.action: Identify the last known good deployment revision for the affected workloads.
steps.verify: Deployment history logs confirm valid rollback target.

steps.action: Prepare and stage the rollback plan, specifying target revisions and rollback steps.
steps.verify: Rollback plan is reviewed and documented in the incident ticket.

steps.action: Temporarily suspend automated deployment pipelines for the affected workloads.
steps.verify: Pipeline status reflects suspension and no new deployments are in progress.

steps.action: Initiate the rollback of workloads to the identified stable revision in the cluster.
steps.verify: Workload deployment status in cluster reflects rollback to intended revision.

steps.action: Monitor application and infrastructure metrics for errors or anomalies post-rollback.
steps.verify: Metrics dashboards show error rates and resource usage return to baseline.

steps.action: Perform functional checks to validate service health and customer impact resolution.
steps.verify: Health checks and synthetic transactions succeed as expected.

steps.action: Communicate rollback status and outcome to stakeholders.
steps.verify: Status update is logged in incident channel and acknowledged by stakeholders.

steps.action: Resume automated deployment pipelines for the affected workloads.
steps.verify: Pipeline status reflects resumed state and new deployments are possible.

steps.action: Document the rollback actions, observations, and lessons learned in the incident ticket.
steps.verify: Incident ticket is updated with a detailed post-rollback summary.

abort_conditions: Manager approval is denied or delayed beyond response window.
Verification of rollback target revision fails.
Cluster health check reveals unsafe environment for rollback.

name: Debug Production Issue

The process involves gathering evidence, analyzing logs and metrics, identifying the root cause, and coordinating with oncall and relevant teams to resolve the problem while minimizing risk to production data.

steps.verify: Incident ticket is updated with acknowledgment and impact details

steps.action: Gather relevant logs from the monitoring system for affected services
steps.verify: Log entries are collected and attached to the incident ticket

steps.action: Review recent deployment and configuration changes related to the payments system
steps.verify: Change logs and deployment records are reviewed and findings noted

steps.action: Examine system metrics for anomalies (latency, error rates, resource utilization)
steps.verify: Metrics dashboard shows evidence of anomaly correlated to the incident

steps.action: Correlate user reports and support tickets with system events
steps.verify: Timeline of user impact is matched with system events in documentation

steps.action: Consult with oncall team members for additional context or recent issues
steps.verify: Oncall communication is logged in the incident ticket

steps.action: Document a clear problem statement based on the evidence collected
steps.verify: Incident ticket contains a concise problem statement

steps.action: Formulate a hypothesis for the root cause based on available data
steps.verify: Root cause hypothesis is documented in the incident ticket

steps.action: Validate hypothesis by cross-referencing additional logs or metrics
steps.verify: Supporting or contradicting evidence is recorded in the ticket

steps.action: Escalate to relevant engineering or SRE teams if outside backend scope
steps.verify: Escalation is reflected in ticket ownership or notifications

steps.action: Summarize findings and recommended remediation steps
steps.verify: Summary and recommendations are added to the incident ticket

steps.action: Communicate status and next steps to stakeholders
steps.verify: Stakeholder communication is logged in the incident record

abort_conditions: Verification of evidence fails to support a root cause
Access to required logs or metrics is denied
Production environment is deemed unsafe for investigation

name: Production IAM Security Audit

steps.verify: Audit scope is documented and approved

steps.verify: List of privileged accounts and justifications is documented

steps.action: Analyze group and role memberships for excessive or anomalous permissions

steps.action: Communicate key findings and next steps to relevant stakeholders
steps.verify: Stakeholders acknowledge receipt of the summary

steps.action: Log any defects or anomalies discovered during testing
steps.verify: Defect tickets are created and tracked in the ticketing system

name: Rollback a Kubernetes Release in Production
description: The site reliability engineer is responding to an alert indicating degraded service after a recent deployment in the production Kubernetes cluster. The intent is to roll back the affected deployment to the previous stable version while ensuring service reliability and compliance with operational constraints. This process is governed by a two-person rule due to its potentially disruptive impact.

steps.action: Acknowledge the alert and notify the incident response channel
steps.verify: Incident response channel shows notification and acknowledgment recorded

steps.action: Review recent deployment history and identify the affected release
steps.verify: Deployment logs and dashboard reflect the recent change and its impact

steps.action: Gather rollback approval from a second authorized engineer
steps.verify: Approval is documented in the incident management system

steps.action: Assess current system health and resource utilization in the cluster
steps.verify: Monitoring dashboard displays real-time health metrics and resource status

steps.action: Identify the previous stable version of the deployment
steps.verify: Release registry or deployment history confirms the stable version

steps.action: Initiate the rollback procedure for the affected deployment
steps.verify: Deployment status in the Kubernetes dashboard transitions to previous version

steps.action: Monitor pod restarts and readiness during rollback
steps.verify: Pod status indicators show all pods for the deployment are running and ready

steps.action: Check service endpoints to confirm traffic is directed to healthy pods
steps.verify: Load balancer or service metrics confirm healthy endpoints

steps.action: Validate core application functionality post-rollback
steps.verify: Application health checks and synthetic tests pass successfully

steps.action: Monitor for recurrence of alerts or errors after rollback
steps.verify: Alerting system shows no new critical alerts related to the deployment

steps.action: Document the rollback action and outcomes in the incident management system
steps.verify: Incident record contains rollback details and resolution notes

steps.action: Communicate rollback completion and status to stakeholders
steps.verify: Stakeholder notification channels confirm receipt of rollback update

abort_conditions: Approval from second authorized engineer is not obtained
System health check reveals unsafe cluster conditions
Rollback verification steps fail to restore service stability

name: Contract Review for Vendor Renewal

steps.verify: Access anomalies are flagged in an audit report.

steps.verify: Inactive accounts are confirmed as disabled or removed.

steps.verify: Compliance gaps are identified and documented.

steps.action: Verify that database access is logged and audit trails are intact.
steps.verify: Logging configuration and retention policies are reviewed and confirmed.

steps.action: Document all findings, risks, and recommended remediations.
steps.verify: Comprehensive audit report is prepared and reviewed.

steps.action: Share audit results with relevant stakeholders, including management and database owners.
steps.verify: Stakeholder acknowledgment or feedback is logged.

steps.action: Initiate remediation requests for identified issues through the appropriate ticketing system.

steps.action: Schedule a follow-up review to confirm remediation actions are completed.
steps.verify: Follow-up date is set and documented.

abort_conditions: Manager approval is not granted.
Access to required audit data is unavailable.
Critical verification step fails or reveals unsafe environment.

name: Scale Application Deployment in Production

steps.action: Check current deployment status and replica count for the affected application.

steps.verify: Manager approval is recorded in the incident log

steps.action: Document initial impact assessment in the incident ticket
steps.verify: Assessment is timestamped and visible in the ticketing system

steps.action: Contain the incident to prevent further impact
steps.verify: Containment actions are logged and system metrics stabilize

steps.action: Gather logs and supporting evidence from the monitoring platform
steps.verify: Log files and evidence are attached to the incident ticket

steps.action: Correlate alerts with recent changes or deployments
steps.verify: Change timeline is documented and cross-referenced in the ticket

steps.action: Engage additional subject matter experts as needed
steps.verify: Expert participation is noted in the incident log

steps.action: Propose remediation actions and seek manager approval for any destructive steps
steps.verify: Remediation plan and approvals are documented in the ticket

steps.action: Execute approved remediation actions
steps.verify: Action completion is logged and system health is re-evaluated

steps.action: Monitor service recovery and validate restoration of normal operations
steps.verify: Monitoring dashboards show recovery and incident is marked as mitigated

steps.action: Communicate status updates to stakeholders at regular intervals
steps.verify: Updates are posted in the incident channel and acknowledged

steps.action: Update incident ticket with summary of actions taken and outcomes
steps.verify: Ticket contains a complete timeline and resolution notes

name: Production Release Deployment
description: A site reliability engineer initiates the deployment of a new software release to the production environment. The process follows established change management practices, with checks at each stage to ensure service stability and compliance. Approval is required before executing any changes that impact production workloads.

steps.action: Review the deployment plan and associated release notes for completeness and accuracy.
steps.verify: Deployment plan and release notes are up-to-date and approved in the documentation system.

steps.action: Check production environment health and capacity for readiness.
steps.verify: Production monitoring dashboards show all systems operational and no capacity warnings.

steps.action: Validate that all automated tests have passed in the staging environment.
steps.verify: CI/CD pipeline reports all tests as successful in staging.

steps.action: Confirm that the rollback plan is documented and accessible.
steps.verify: Rollback procedures are reviewed and linked in the deployment documentation.

steps.action: Request manager approval for the production deployment.
steps.verify: Manager approval is recorded in the change management system.

steps.action: Announce the scheduled deployment window to stakeholders.
steps.verify: Deployment notice is posted in the designated communication channel.

steps.action: Place production system into maintenance or pre-deployment state as required.
steps.verify: Maintenance status is visible on status dashboards and communicated to users.

steps.action: Initiate the deployment process using the CI/CD system.
steps.verify: CI/CD system logs show deployment process has started.

steps.action: Monitor deployment progress and check for errors or warnings.
steps.verify: Deployment logs and dashboards show no critical errors during rollout.

steps.action: Run post-deployment health checks and smoke tests.
steps.verify: Health checks and smoke test results are successful and logged.

steps.action: Remove maintenance status and notify stakeholders of deployment completion.
steps.verify: System is restored to normal operation and stakeholders receive confirmation.

steps.action: Update deployment records with results and any lessons learned.
steps.verify: Deployment records are updated in the change management or documentation system.

abort_conditions: Manager approval is denied or delayed beyond the scheduled window.
Critical error detected in health checks or deployment logs.
Production environment is not in a safe or ready state for deployment.

name: Regression Testing on Staging Release
description: A QA engineer performs regression testing on a new release candidate in the staging environment. The goal is to ensure that recent code changes have not negatively impacted existing functionalities. All results are documented, and critical issues are reported before deployment approval.

steps.action: Review the release notes and test plan for the staging build
steps.verify: Test plan is updated and release notes are accessible in documentation

steps.action: Access the staging environment using authorized credentials
steps.verify: Successful login and access to staging system is logged

steps.action: Execute automated regression test suite on the new build
steps.verify: Automated test results are recorded and available in CI/CD reports

steps.action: Manually validate critical user flows as described in the test plan
steps.verify: Manual test cases are marked complete in the test management tool

steps.action: Log any defects or anomalies identified during testing
steps.verify: Defects are recorded in the tracking system with relevant details

steps.action: Re-test resolved defects in the staging environment
steps.verify: Defect status is updated to 'verified' upon successful retest

steps.action: Document test results and overall quality assessment
steps.verify: Test summary report is available for stakeholder review

steps.action: Communicate testing status and any blocking issues to the release manager
steps.verify: Release manager acknowledges receipt of status update

steps.action: Archive test artifacts and evidence in the designated repository
steps.verify: Artifacts are accessible in the QA documentation area

abort_conditions: Automated or manual tests reveal critical blocking issues
Staging environment is unavailable or unstable
Test plan or release notes are incomplete or missing

name: Perform Database Schema Migration
description: A platform engineer initiates a schema migration on the production database as part of routine maintenance. The process includes planning, communication, execution, and validation to ensure minimal disruption and data integrity.

steps.action: Review and document the planned schema changes with relevant stakeholders.
steps.verify: Stakeholder approval documented and migration plan reviewed.

steps.action: Obtain formal manager approval for the migration window.
steps.verify: Approval confirmation received and recorded.

steps.action: Communicate the scheduled migration to affected teams and update status channels.
steps.verify: Notification acknowledged by stakeholders and status updated.

steps.action: Perform a backup of the production database prior to migration.
steps.verify: Backup completion verified and backup integrity confirmed.

steps.action: Apply the schema migration to a staging environment for validation.
steps.verify: Staging environment reflects intended schema changes without errors.

steps.action: Validate key application workflows in staging post-migration.
steps.verify: Test results show no breakages or data integrity issues.

steps.action: Schedule a maintenance window for the production schema migration.
steps.verify: Maintenance window published and acknowledged by teams.

steps.action: Disable non-essential database access during the migration window.
steps.verify: Access logs confirm restricted connections.

steps.action: Apply the schema migration to the production database.
steps.verify: Schema version updated and migration logs confirm successful execution.

steps.action: Monitor application and database metrics for anomalies post-migration.
steps.verify: Metrics remain within expected thresholds and no new alerts are triggered.

steps.action: Re-enable previously disabled database access.
steps.verify: Access restored and connection logs reflect normal activity.

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of migration completion and system status.
steps.verify: Stakeholder acknowledgments received and incident channels updated.

steps.action: Document the migration outcome, including any issues and resolutions.
steps.verify: Migration report filed and accessible in documentation systems.

abort_conditions: Manager approval is denied or delayed beyond maintenance window.
Backup or staging validation fails.
Unexpected anomalies detected during migration execution.

name: Track Delivery Milestone Progress
description: A project manager reviews the progress of key delivery milestones for an active project. The manager checks status updates, verifies completion evidence, and documents any schedule or resource risks. This process ensures transparent reporting and enables timely escalation of issues.

steps.action: Access the project documentation repository
steps.verify: Project workspace is accessible and up to date

steps.action: Review the latest project timeline and milestone list
steps.verify: Milestone schedule is present and reflects current targets

steps.action: Check status updates for each active milestone
steps.verify: Status fields for milestones are populated and current

steps.action: Collect evidence of milestone deliverable completion
steps.verify: Supporting documents or links for completed milestones are attached

steps.action: Identify any overdue milestones or schedule slippage
steps.verify: Overdue items are highlighted in the milestone tracker

steps.action: Note resource constraints or blockers impacting delivery
steps.verify: Risks and blockers are documented in the project risk log

steps.action: Summarize progress and risks in a status update entry
steps.verify: New status update is timestamped and visible in the documentation

steps.action: Share the updated milestone progress report with the project team
steps.verify: Notification or summary is sent to all relevant team members

abort_conditions: Project documentation is inaccessible
Milestone status data is missing or outdated
Material discrepancies are detected in milestone evidence

name: Restore Production Database from Backup
description: A site reliability engineer is restoring the production database from the latest backup due to a critical incident. This operation is urgent, involves confidential data, and requires following destructive change controls with security approval. The process ensures business continuity and data integrity for the platform.

steps.action: Acknowledge the alert indicating database corruption or data loss
steps.verify: Incident ticket is updated to acknowledged state

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of planned database restore and expected downtime
steps.verify: Stakeholder notification is visible in incident communication channel

steps.action: Review and confirm the scope of data loss and affected services
steps.verify: Incident documentation reflects validated impact assessment

steps.action: Obtain security approval for destructive database restore operation
steps.verify: Approval is recorded in change management or ticketing system

steps.action: Identify and select the most recent valid backup for the production database
steps.verify: Backup selection is documented in the incident ticket

steps.action: Take a snapshot of the current (corrupted) database state for post-mortem analysis
steps.verify: Snapshot confirmation is logged in backup inventory

steps.action: Isolate database access for all non-essential users and systems
steps.verify: Access logs show only authorized restore operations in progress

steps.action: Initiate the database restore process using the selected backup
steps.verify: Restore progress is visible in monitoring or backup dashboard

steps.action: Monitor restore process for errors or integrity warnings
steps.verify: No critical errors are reported in restore logs

steps.action: Run post-restore integrity and consistency checks on the database
steps.verify: Integrity check results are attached to the incident ticket

steps.action: Re-enable database access for essential services
steps.verify: Service status dashboard shows application connectivity restored

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of successful database restoration and service recovery
steps.verify: Stakeholder communication is updated with recovery confirmation

steps.action: Document actions taken, lessons learned, and update disaster recovery runbook
steps.verify: Incident post-mortem and documentation are completed and accessible

steps.action: Schedule a follow-up review to improve backup and recovery processes
steps.verify: Follow-up task is created in team planning system

abort_conditions: Approval for destructive restore is denied
Backup integrity cannot be verified
Critical errors occur during restore process

name: Rollback a Faulty Production Deployment
description: A site reliability engineer needs to rollback a recent Kubernetes deployment after a critical production issue was detected. The process requires careful verification at each stage to ensure system stability and minimal user impact. This operation is performed with production admin privileges and follows safety and approval protocols.

steps.action: Review the production incident details and determine the need for rollback
steps.verify: Incident ticket updated with rollback decision and rationale

steps.action: Request manager approval to proceed with the rollback operation
steps.verify: Approval status documented in change management system

steps.action: Identify the last known good deployment version in the deployment history
steps.verify: Deployment history log shows selected rollback target

steps.action: Prepare a rollback plan including expected impact and monitoring checkpoints
steps.verify: Rollback plan reviewed and acknowledged by relevant stakeholders

steps.action: Isolate the affected Kubernetes namespace or deployment to minimize collateral impact
steps.verify: Targeted namespace or deployment flagged as isolated in cluster dashboard

steps.action: Initiate the rollback to the previously stable deployment version
steps.verify: Cluster events show rollback operation initiated

steps.action: Monitor pod restarts and resource health during rollback
steps.verify: Pod status and resource metrics indicate healthy state

steps.action: Check application logs for errors or anomalies post-rollback
steps.verify: Application logs show resolution of the original issue

steps.action: Validate service endpoints for correct functionality and user experience
steps.verify: Service endpoints pass health checks and user flows are operational

steps.action: Update incident and change management tickets with rollback status and observations
steps.verify: Tickets reflect rollback completion and system status

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of rollback completion and system recovery
steps.verify: Stakeholder notifications acknowledged and documented

steps.action: Schedule a follow-up review to analyze root cause and preventive measures
steps.verify: Follow-up review scheduled in team calendar

abort_conditions: Manager approval is denied

name: Execute Release Smoke Tests
description: A QA engineer initiates and monitors smoke tests on the staging environment prior to a scheduled release. This process ensures that critical workflows function as expected and helps identify blocking issues before production deployment.

name: Routine Network Maintenance
description: A site reliability engineer performs scheduled maintenance tasks to ensure network health and reliability in the production environment. This process includes reviewing network status, applying safe configuration changes, and validating connectivity. All actions are taken with high caution to minimize risk to production systems.

steps.action: Review scheduled maintenance window and planned changes for the network.
steps.verify: Maintenance plan is documented and communicated to relevant teams.

steps.action: Notify stakeholders of upcoming maintenance activities and expected impact.
steps.verify: Notification is acknowledged by stakeholders or logged in communication channels.

steps.action: Check current network health metrics and baseline performance.
steps.verify: Baseline metrics are recorded from monitoring dashboards.

steps.action: Confirm all backup and rollback measures are in place for network configurations.
